1. Open Nvidia control panel and create (2) Custom resolutions
The first one is 1920 x 1080 progressive at 60 hz The next is 1916 x 1080 progressive at 60 hz 2. Select the first 1920 x 1080 p at 60 under the new custom resolution and apply

In Nvidia control panel, navigate to Adjust desktop size and position
Select NO Scaling option and make panel look like below, apply settings . Launch Crysis, Crysis 2 or Crysis Warhead and under Graphics settings, select the resolution 1916x1080 and apply

Your screen should default to 1080p at 60 hz, you can check the info on your HDTV and it should say 1920x1080 at 60hz rather than 24hz.
There you go
